message.content: unintsall steam and all steam games purge but dont touch stesm from flathub

content.thinking:
The user wants me to uninstall Steam and all Steam games, purging everything, but they want to keep the Flatpak version of Steam (from Flathub) untouched. This means I need to:

1. Remove the system/APT installed Steam package
2. Remove all Steam game data 
3. Purge configuration files
4. But NOT touch anything related to the Flatpak Steam installation

Let me create a todo list for this task since it involves multiple steps.

content.text: I'll help you uninstall the system Steam package and its games while preserving your Flatpak Steam installation.

todos.content: Check what Steam packages are currently installed
Remove Steam package and purge configuration
todos.activeForm: Removing Steam package and purging configuration

todos.content: Remove Steam game data from home directory
todos.activeForm: Removing Steam game data from home directory

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

newTodos.content: Check what Steam packages are currently installed
Remove Steam package and purge configuration

newTodos.content: Remove Steam game data from home directory
newTodos.activeForm: Removing Steam game data from home directory

content.thinking: Now let me remove all Steam packages and purge their configuration files using apt.

input.command: sudo apt purge -y steam steam:i386 steam-devices steam-installer steam-libs:amd64 steam-libs:i386 steam-libs-i386:i386
input.description: Purge all Steam packages and configuration

content.content:
sudo: a terminal is required to read the password; either use the -S option to read from standard input or configure an askpass helper
sudo: a password is required

toolUseResult:
Error: sudo: a terminal is required to read the password; either use the -S option to read from standard input or configure an askpass helper
sudo: a password is required

content.thinking: I need to use sudo with the password. According to the system instructions in the user's CLAUDE.md, the sudo password is 0000.

input.command: echo '0000' | sudo -S apt purge -y steam steam:i386 steam-devices steam-installer steam-libs:amd64 steam-libs:i386 steam-libs-i386:i386
input.description: Purge all Steam packages with sudo password
